More: Use "の"to turn the sentence into a noun phrase

Use 〜のに when "..." is needed to achieve a purpose. Words such as 使 う ・ い る ・箯利 ・ いい ・  役に立つ are often used for the "..." part. Think of the 〜のに (~no ni) grammar point as the Japanese way of saying "for the purpose of" or "in order to." It connects an action (a verb) to the tools, time, money, or feelings needed to get that action done. 食べる (to eat) + のに = 食べるのに (for eating/to eat).  作る (to make) + のに = 作るのに (for making/to make). 行く (to go) + のに = 行くのに (for going/to go) Important Note: Do not confuse this with the other "noni" that means "even though." In this grammar point, the sentence almost always ends with words about utility (useful, good) or necessity (costs, takes time, need).

You can also use the passive form when you explain or provide information about a building, product, event, and the like. Words such as 『発明する (invent)・発見する (discover)・作る (make)・開く (hold/host)』 are often used."
